title: rhinanthus alpinus subsp. carinthiacus
intro: Rhinanthus alpinus subsp. carinthiacus, also known as the Alpine Rampion or Alpen-Ragwurz, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the broomrape family, Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, which means it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing surrounding plant species, while still photosynthesizing like other plants. Rhinanthus alpinus subsp. carinthiacus has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
